background_concepts.concept: Call Detail Records (CDRs) as mobility proxy
background_concepts.why_needed: Several readings use mobile phone call records to infer population movement but assume the reader knows what CDRs are, how cell-tower pings approximate location, and their sampling/representativeness biases.

suggested_resources.title: Making the Most of Mobility Data (CDRs): A Guide for Policymakers
What are Call Detail Records (CDR data)? — FlowGeek

background_concepts.why_needed: The agent-based and spatiotemporal disease-spread papers build on standard susceptible-exposed-infectious-recovered dynamics as the underlying disease process, without explaining the compartments or transition rates.

The Scott nowcasting reading presents state-space/structural time-series forecasting and 'predicting the present' without introducing the underlying state-space decomposition or spike-and-slab regression basics assumed of the reader.

background_concepts.concept: Influence maximization / seed-set selection on networks
background_concepts.why_needed: The fair-vaccination paper frames vaccine promotion as choosing a 'seed set' to maximize spread over a social/contact network, assuming familiarity with diffusion models (independent cascade / linear threshold) and the greedy submodular approximation.

suggested_resources.title: WSDM 2018 Tutorial on Influence Maximization in Online Social Networks

domain_name: Restless Bandits and Resource Allocation for Public Health Outreach

background_concepts.concept: Restless multi-armed bandits and the Whittle index
background_concepts.why_needed: These papers assume the reader understands RMABs, the passive/active arm formulation, indexability, and the Whittle index policy used to prioritize which beneficiaries to allocate limited health-worker attention to.

suggested_resources.title: Markovian restless bandits and index policies: A review (intro sections)

domain_name: AI-Assisted Medical Imaging Diagnosis in Low-Resource Settings

background_concepts.concept: Convolutional neural networks for medical image classification
background_concepts.why_needed: The chest X-ray silicosis/pneumoconiosis and cancer-screening papers assume the reader understands CNN building blocks (convolution, pooling, fully-connected layers, AUC evaluation) used to classify medical images.

suggested_resources.title: A practical introduction to convolutional neural networks for medical professionals
Deep learning: CNNs for medical image classification (IMAIOS)

background_concepts.why_needed: SHIELD is described as a 'self-supervised hierarchical imaging framework,' assuming the reader knows how models learn representations from unlabeled images (pretext tasks, contrastive learning), which is essential in label-scarce clinical settings.

suggested_resources.title: Self-Supervised Learning: Self-Prediction and Contrastive Learning (NeurIPS 2021 tutorial slides)

domain_name: Maternal, Child, and Reproductive Health Digital Systems

background_concepts.concept: Causal inference from observational data (potential outcomes)
background_concepts.why_needed: The contraceptive-discontinuation paper applies causal inference to survey data, assuming familiarity with the potential-outcomes framework, counterfactuals, and confounding rather than teaching them.

suggested_resources.title: Causal Inference and Potential Outcomes (UCL PUBL0050 lecture notes)
Causal inference and effect estimation using observational data (glossary)

background_concepts.concept: Satellite remote sensing and multispectral imagery
background_concepts.why_needed: The emission-mapping, kiln-classifier, and wildfire-smoke papers rely on multispectral and geostationary satellite bands, spatial/spectral/temporal resolution tradeoffs, and image preprocessing that are assumed rather than explained.

suggested_resources.title: Fundamentals of Remote Sensing (NASA ARSET self-paced course)
Introduction to Spatial and Spectral Resolution: Multispectral Imagery (Earth Lab)

domain_name: Clean Cooking and Household Energy Transitions

background_concepts.why_needed: The 'theory of change for cleaner cooking' paper explicitly builds a Health Belief Model for service design, assuming the reader knows its constructs (perceived susceptibility, severity, benefits, barriers, cues to action, self-efficacy).

suggested_resources.title: The Health Belief Model — Methods for Stress Management (Pressbooks)
Understanding the Health Belief Model — A Primer

domain_name: Designing for Stigmatized and Marginalized Care Recipients
Secure and Agile mHealth Deployment in the Field
Health Information System (HIS/DHIS2) Implementation in LMICs

background_concepts.why_needed: Readings in this domain assume familiarity with DHIS2's architecture, aggregate vs. case-based data, and the HISP implementation ecosystem.

domain_name: mHealth Behavior Change and Frontline Health Worker Support
AI/ML for Clinical Prediction and Health Equity

background_concepts.why_needed: The subject-wise clustering and online contrastive-learning papers assume the reader understands how contrastive objectives (positive/negative pairs, embedding space) learn representations from largely unlabeled data.

domain_name: Digital Technology in Pandemic Response and Risk Communication
